name: Lightspeed Retail API
what_it_does: Cloud POS REST API for retail stores covering inventory, sales, customers, employees, and reporting.
use_cases: Automate inventory replenishment based on sales velocity
Sync product catalog across multiple store locations
Generate sales and margin reports for retail analytics
Manage customer loyalty and purchase history
Integrate retail POS data with ERP or accounting systems
not_for: Restaurant or food service operations (use Toast or Square for Restaurants)
Enterprise retail chains needing on-premise deployment
High-frequency trading or real-time stock systems
best_when: Building retail automation agents for bike shops, apparel, or specialty retail stores that use Lightspeed as their POS
avoid_when: You need a sandbox environment without a paid merchant account, or you operate in food service

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with per-scope authorization for employee, inventory, and reporting data; token refresh required

pricing.paid_tiers: Starts ~$89/month per location; API access included with subscription
pricing.notes: Requires active Lightspeed merchant subscription; no standalone API-only plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors returned as JSON with code and message fields; not always actionable for agents
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported on order creation; not universal across endpoints
known_agent_gotchas: Requires live merchant account to test — no free sandbox
OAuth token refresh must be handled by the agent
Rate limits per 5-minute window can surprise agents doing bulk operations
Community SDKs may lag behind API changes

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with scoped tokens; PCI-DSS compliant; GDPR data residency options available
